description: Standard HTTP datasource fields shared by the grafana-plugin-sdk-go HTTP client (`backend/httpclient/options.go` — `Options`, `BasicAuthOptions`, `TLSOptions`, `TimeoutOptions`) and the Grafana UI HTTP settings editor (`@grafana/ui` `DataSourceHttpSettings`, composed of `BasicAuthSettings`, `HttpProxySettings`, `TLSAuthSettings`, `CustomHeadersSettings`, `SecureSocksProxySettings`). Storage shape mirrors the canonical `DataSourceSettings` model (root-level `url`/`access`/`basicAuth*`/`withCredentials`, `jsonData.*`, `secureJsonData.*`).

fields.label: URL
fields.description: Complete HTTP URL of the datasource (e.g. https://your-server:8080).

help.markdown: Stored at the root `url` field of the `DataSourceSettings` model. Rendered by `DataSourceHttpSettings` as the first `URL` input. The Grafana UI validates the value against `^(ftp|http|https)://...`.

fields.label: Access
fields.description: Controls how requests to the datasource are routed. `proxy` (Server) sends requests through the Grafana backend; `direct` (Browser) sends them straight from the browser and is subject to CORS.

help.markdown: Stored at the root `access` field of the `DataSourceSettings` model. The `direct` (Browser) mode is deprecated in modern Grafana — most datasources should leave this as `proxy`.

fields.label: Allowed cookies
fields.description: Cookies, by name, that the Grafana proxy should forward to the datasource. The Grafana proxy strips all cookies by default.

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.keepCookies` as an array of cookie names. Only meaningful when `access == 'proxy'`.

fields.label: Timeout
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds. Maps to `TimeoutOptions.Timeout` in the plugin SDK HTTP client.

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.timeout`. Forwarded to the SDK via `httpclient.Options.Timeouts.Timeout` (`backend/httpclient/options.go`). The SDK default (`DefaultTimeoutOptions.Timeout`) is 30 seconds.

fields.label: Basic auth
fields.description: Enable HTTP Basic authentication. When enabled, the SDK populates `httpclient.Options.BasicAuth` from `basicAuthUser` / `secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword`.

help.markdown: Stored at the root `basicAuth` field of the `DataSourceSettings` model. Rendered as a toggle inside the `Auth` section of `DataSourceHttpSettings`. When true, the `Basic Auth Details` subsection (`BasicAuthSettings`) is shown.
Stored at the root `basicAuthUser` field. Forwarded to the SDK as `httpclient.BasicAuthOptions.User`.
Stored in `secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword`. Forwarded to the SDK as `httpclient.BasicAuthOptions.Password`.

fields.label: With Credentials
fields.description: Whether credentials such as cookies or auth headers should be sent with cross-site requests.

help.markdown: Stored at the root `withCredentials` field of the `DataSourceSettings` model. Applies to the browser `fetch` call in `direct` access mode and to the Grafana proxy's outbound request in `proxy` mode.

fields.label: TLS Client Auth
fields.description: Enable mTLS client authentication. When enabled, `secureJsonData.tlsClientCert` and `secureJsonData.tlsClientKey` are sent to the upstream server.

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.tlsAuth`. Rendered as the `TLS Client Auth` toggle in `HttpProxySettings`. When true the `TLSAuthSettings` subsection exposes `ServerName`, `Client Cert`, and `Client Key`.

fields.label: With CA Cert
fields.description: Enable verification against a custom CA certificate (`secureJsonData.tlsCACert`). Needed for verifying self-signed TLS certs.

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert`. Rendered as the `With CA Cert` toggle in `HttpProxySettings`. When true the `TLSAuthSettings` subsection exposes the `CA Cert` input.

fields.label: Skip TLS Verify
fields.description: Skip verification of the upstream TLS certificate. Forwarded to the SDK as `TLSOptions.InsecureSkipVerify`.

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.tlsSkipVerify`. Rendered as the `Skip TLS Verify` toggle in `HttpProxySettings`.

fields.label: Server Name
fields.description: Server name used for TLS SNI / certificate verification. Forwarded to the SDK as `TLSOptions.ServerName`.

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.serverName`. Rendered in `TLSAuthSettings` when `tlsAuth == true`.

fields.label: CA Cert
fields.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate used to verify the upstream TLS certificate. Forwarded to the SDK as `TLSOptions.CACertificate`.

help.markdown: Stored in `secureJsonData.tlsCACert`. Rendered as a textarea in `TLSAuthSettings` when `tlsAuthWithCACert == true`.

fields.label: Client Cert
fields.description: PEM-encoded client certificate used for mTLS. Forwarded to the SDK as `TLSOptions.ClientCertificate`.

help.markdown: Stored in `secureJsonData.tlsClientCert`. Rendered as a textarea in `TLSAuthSettings` when `tlsAuth == true`.

fields.label: Client Key
fields.description: PEM-encoded private key paired with `tlsClientCert`. Forwarded to the SDK as `TLSOptions.ClientKey`.

help.markdown: Stored in `secureJsonData.tlsClientKey`. Rendered as a textarea in `TLSAuthSettings` when `tlsAuth == true`.

fields.label: Forward OAuth Identity
fields.description: Forward the signed-in user's upstream OAuth identity (access token) to the datasource.

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.oauthPassThru`. Rendered as the `Forward OAuth Identity` toggle in `HttpProxySettings` (only when the host plugin opts in via `showForwardOAuthIdentityOption`). Mutually exclusive with Azure Authentication in the Grafana UI.

fields.label: SigV4 auth
fields.description: Enable AWS SigV4 request signing. Forwarded to the SDK as `httpclient.Options.SigV4` (see `SigV4Config`).

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.sigV4Auth`. Rendered as the `SigV4 auth` toggle in `DataSourceHttpSettings` (only when the host plugin opts in via `sigV4AuthToggleEnabled`). The SigV4 credential fields themselves come from the AWS SDK pack (`aws_sdk_settings`).

fields.label: Secure Socks Proxy
fields.description: Route datasource traffic through Grafana's Secure Socks Proxy (PDC).

help.markdown: Stored at `jsonData.enableSecureSocksProxy`. Rendered by `SecureSocksProxySettings` when the host Grafana instance has `secureSocksDSProxyEnabled`.
Stored at `jsonData.secureSocksProxyUsername`. Optional username for the Secure Socks Proxy connection.
Stored in `secureJsonData.secureSocksProxyPassword`. Optional password for the Secure Socks Proxy connection.

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Forwarded to the SDK as `httpclient.Options.Header`.

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

help.markdown: Stored as the legacy indexed pair `jsonData.httpHeaderName{N}` / `secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue{N}` (starting at index 1) — the shape `CustomHeadersSettings` writes and the SDK reads when populating `httpclient.Options.Header`.
